req.title: Overdraft protection (idempotency variant)
req.statement: An overdraft shall trigger a draw from the linked savings account up to the linked account's available balance; otherwise the transaction shall be declined. The operation must be idempotent: duplicate submission with the same Idempotency-Key must return the original result.

doc.reasoning: The requirement covers four distinct behaviours: (1) successful overdraft draw from linked savings, (2) decline when linked savings balance is insufficient, (3) boundary where savings balance exactly equals the overdraft shortfall, and (4) idempotency of duplicate submissions. EP partitions the space into 'sufficient linked balance' vs 'insufficient linked balance'. BVA targets the exact-match boundary on savings balance. STC tracks the account state transition from 'checking overdrawn' through 'savings debited' to 'fee assessed'. NEG covers the decline path. DT resolves the combination of checking deficit × savings availability. Each test uses a unique Idempotency-Key generated at runtime to remain self-contained and deterministic. Fixtures provide pre-seeded account state via the <setup_accounts> and <teardown_accounts> helpers, keeping executable bodies within 40 lines.

tests.name: test_overdraft_successful_draw_from_linked_savings
preconditions: Customer has a checking account with balance $50.00
Customer has a linked savings account with balance $200.00
Overdraft protection is active on the checking account
actions: POST /overdraft with debit amount $120.00 and a unique Idempotency-Key
expected: overdraft fee assessed and reflected in response
checking account balance is $0.00 after transaction
trace: R-FIN-087: linked savings debited when debit exceeds checking balance
R-FIN-087: fee assessed on overdraft event
tests.executable:
import uuid
import pytest
from <fixture> import api_client, setup_accounts, teardown_accounts

@pytest.fixture(autouse=True)
def account_state(setup_accounts, teardown_accounts):
    setup_accounts(checking_balance=50_00, savings_balance=200_00)
    yield
    teardown_accounts()

def test_overdraft_successful_draw_from_linked_savings(api_client):
    idem_key = str(uuid.uuid4())
    payload = {"amount": 120_00, "currency": "USD"}
    headers = {"Idempotency-Key": idem_key}

    resp = api_client.post("/overdraft", json=payload, headers=headers)

    assert resp.status_code == 200
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["status"] == "approved"
    assert body["savings_debited"] == 70_00
    assert body["overdraft_fee"] > 0
    assert body["checking_balance"] == 0

tests.name: test_overdraft_declined_when_linked_savings_insufficient
preconditions: Customer has a checking account with balance $10.00
Customer has a linked savings account with balance $5.00
Overdraft protection is active on the checking account
actions: POST /overdraft with debit amount $50.00 and a unique Idempotency-Key
expected: response body contains decline_reason indicating insufficient linked balance
trace: R-FIN-087: transaction shall be declined when linked account balance is insufficient
tests.executable:
import uuid
import pytest
from <fixture> import api_client, setup_accounts, teardown_accounts

@pytest.fixture(autouse=True)
def account_state(setup_accounts, teardown_accounts):
    setup_accounts(checking_balance=10_00, savings_balance=5_00)
    yield
    teardown_accounts()

def test_overdraft_declined_when_linked_savings_insufficient(api_client):
    idem_key = str(uuid.uuid4())
    payload = {"amount": 50_00, "currency": "USD"}
    headers = {"Idempotency-Key": idem_key}

    resp = api_client.post("/overdraft", json=payload, headers=headers)

    assert resp.status_code in (402, 422)
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["status"] == "declined"
    assert "insufficient" in body["decline_reason"].lower()
    balances = api_client.get("/accounts/balances").json()
    assert balances["savings"] == 5_00
    assert balances["checking"] == 10_00

tests.name: test_overdraft_boundary_savings_exactly_covers_shortfall
preconditions: Customer has a checking account with balance $30.00
Customer has a linked savings account with balance $70.00
Overdraft shortfall for a $100.00 debit is exactly $70.00
Overdraft protection is active on the checking account
actions: POST /overdraft with debit amount $100.00 and a unique Idempotency-Key
expected: savings account debited by exactly $70.00 leaving $0.00 balance
trace: R-FIN-087: draw up to linked account's available balance — exact boundary where savings balance equals shortfall

@pytest.fixture(autouse=True)
def account_state(setup_accounts, teardown_accounts):
    setup_accounts(checking_balance=30_00, savings_balance=70_00)
    yield
    teardown_accounts()

def test_overdraft_boundary_savings_exactly_covers_shortfall(api_client):
    idem_key = str(uuid.uuid4())
    payload = {"amount": 100_00, "currency": "USD"}
    headers = {"Idempotency-Key": idem_key}

    resp = api_client.post("/overdraft", json=payload, headers=headers)

tests.name: test_overdraft_idempotent_duplicate_submission_returns_original_result
preconditions: Customer has a checking account with balance $50.00
Customer has a linked savings account with balance $200.00
Overdraft protection is active on the checking account
actions: POST /overdraft with debit amount $120.00 and a fixed Idempotency-Key (first submission)
POST /overdraft with identical payload and the same Idempotency-Key (duplicate submission)
expected: savings account is debited only once (not twice)
system state after duplicate call equals state after first call
trace: R-FIN-087: duplicate submission with the same Idempotency-Key must return the original result
tests.executable:
import uuid
import pytest
from <fixture> import api_client, setup_accounts, teardown_accounts

@pytest.fixture(autouse=True)
def account_state(setup_accounts, teardown_accounts):
    setup_accounts(checking_balance=50_00, savings_balance=200_00)
    yield
    teardown_accounts()

def test_overdraft_idempotent_duplicate_submission_returns_original_result(api_client):
    idem_key = str(uuid.uuid4())
    payload = {"amount": 120_00, "currency": "USD"}
    headers = {"Idempotency-Key": idem_key}

    resp1 = api_client.post("/overdraft", json=payload, headers=headers)
    resp2 = api_client.post("/overdraft", json=payload, headers=headers)

    assert resp1.status_code == 200
    assert resp2.status_code == 200
    assert resp1.json() == resp2.json()
    balances = api_client.get("/accounts/balances").json()
    assert balances["savings"] == 130_00
    assert balances["checking"] == 0

tests.name: test_overdraft_decision_table_no_linked_account
preconditions: Customer has a checking account with balance $20.00
No savings account is linked to the checking account
actions: POST /overdraft with debit amount $80.00 and a unique Idempotency-Key
expected: response body contains decline_reason indicating no linked account
trace: R-FIN-087: precondition 'overdraft linked' not met — decision table rule: no linked account => decline
tests.executable:
import uuid
import pytest
from <fixture> import api_client, setup_accounts_no_link, teardown_accounts

@pytest.fixture(autouse=True)
def account_state(setup_accounts_no_link, teardown_accounts):
    setup_accounts_no_link(checking_balance=20_00)
    yield
    teardown_accounts()

def test_overdraft_decision_table_no_linked_account(api_client):
    idem_key = str(uuid.uuid4())
    payload = {"amount": 80_00, "currency": "USD"}
    headers = {"Idempotency-Key": idem_key}

    resp = api_client.post("/overdraft", json=payload, headers=headers)

    assert resp.status_code in (404, 422)
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["status"] == "declined"
    assert "linked" in body["decline_reason"].lower()
    balances = api_client.get("/accounts/balances").json()
    assert balances["checking"] == 20_00
